items.headline: Wall Street Opens Muted After Monday Rally, Fed Meeting and Peace Deal in Focus
items.summary: U.S. equities opened largely flat on Tuesday, consolidating gains from Monday's session. Investors are closely monitoring the Federal Reserve's policy meeting, which begins today, and the implications of a preliminary peace agreement between the U.S. and Iran. The peace deal had previously eased Middle East tensions and pushed oil prices lower, contributing to a positive market sentiment.

items.statContext: Closed Monday +0.9% (468.77 pts). Up 0.78% early Tuesday.

items.headline: SpaceX Soars on Acquisition News
items.summary: SpaceX surged in early trading after confirming its acquisition of AI coding startup Anysphere for $60 billion. This follows its recent record-breaking IPO.

items.headline: Western Digital Gains Premarket
items.summary: Western Digital saw significant gains in premarket trading.

items.headline: Qualcomm Rises on Acquisition Talks
items.summary: Qualcomm's shares rose amid reports of ongoing talks to acquire AI chip startup Tenstorrent for up to $10 billion.

items.headline: Dave & Buster's Tumbles on Missed Earnings
items.summary: Dave & Buster's Entertainment tumbled after reporting weaker-than-expected first-quarter earnings and revenue.

items.headline: Huntsman Drops in Premarket
items.summary: Huntsman experienced a notable decline in premarket trading.

items.headline: Hilton Worldwide Holdings Falls
items.summary: Hilton Worldwide Holdings shares fell in premarket activity.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Begins Key Policy Meeting
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's two-day policy meeting, the first under new Chairman Kevin Warsh, commences today. Investors anticipate the interest rate decision and updated economic projections, to be released on Wednesday. The Fed is widely expected to hold interest rates steady.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Peace Agreement Drives Oil Prices Down
items.summary: A preliminary peace agreement between the United States and Iran has significantly eased Middle East tensions and contributed to a sharp decline in crude oil prices. This development has been a major factor in improving overall market sentiment.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data Released
items.summary: The NY Empire State Manufacturing Index for June fell to 5.7, missing expectations, while industrial production in May increased by a modest 0.1%. Capacity utilization for May came in at 76.2%.

items.headline: Technology and Communication Services Lead Rally, Energy Lags
items.summary: On Monday, the Information Technology (XLK), Communication Services (XLC), and Consumer Discretionary (XLY) sectors saw significant gains, rising 3.4%, 2.4%, and 1.9% respectively. Conversely, the Energy sector (XLE) was a notable laggard, falling 3.6%. AI and chip-linked stocks were prominent leaders.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Earnings Today
items.summary: Several companies are scheduled to release their earnings reports today. Before the bell, Vince Holding ($VNCE), Waterdrop ($WDH), Uxin ($UXIN), and John Wiley & Sons ($WLY, $WLYB) are expected to report. After market close, La-Z-Boy ($LZB) and 17 Education & Technology ($YQ) are anticipated to provide their quarterly updates.
